name: Valle del Lago - L'Outeiro Climb
name_original: Valle de Lago desde Pola de Somiedo por el Parque Natural de Somiedo
description: Cycle the moderate Valle del Lago - L'Outeiro Climb, a 11.6-mile route in Somiedo Natural Park with 1923 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: No traffic road cycling routes in Somiedo navigate a dramatic landscape of rugged mountains, lush valleys, and pristine glacial lakes, situated within a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The region features elevations ranging from 400 to 2,200 meters above sea level, offering a dynamic environment for cyclists. Varied topography includes five principal river valleys and extensive beech forests, providing diverse terrain for road cycling. Best no traffic road cycling routes in Somiedo The most popular no traffic road cycling route is Alto de la Farrapona Trail, a 23.3 miles (37.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 25 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and offers views of high peaks and glacial lakes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Valle del Lago - L'Outeiro Climb, a moderate 11.6 miles (18.7 km) path. This route provides a scenic experience through the park's valleys, including the largest lake in Asturias. Local road cyclists also love the Pola de Somiedo – Restaurante Casa Cobrana loop from Somiedo, a 10.6 miles (17.1 km) trail leading through traditional villages and along the Somiedo river, often completed in about 1 hour 8 minutes. Road cycling in Somiedo is defined by rugged mountains, pristine glacial lakes, and rich beech forests, with routes offering options for moderate rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Somiedo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Somiedo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic road cycling routes are available in Somiedo?
A: Somiedo offers a selection of dedicated no-traffic road cycling routes. This guide features 4 such routes, ranging from moderate to difficult, ensuring a quiet and scenic experience through the natural park.

Q: What is the best time of year for traffic-free road cycling in Somiedo?
A: The best season for road cycling in Somiedo is typically from April to November. During these months, the weather is generally more favorable, and key routes like the climb to Puerto de Somiedo are open and accessible. Winter cycling can be challenging due to snow and colder conditions at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any challenging no-traffic road cycling routes in Somiedo?
A: Yes, Somiedo is known for its challenging climbs and significant elevation changes. A notable difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1080322663/alto-de-la-farrapona-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Farrapona Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 37.6 km with over 1,130 meters of elevation gain, offering a demanding ride with spectacular views of the Saliencia lakes.

Q: Are there any moderate no-traffic road cycling options for less experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. While Somiedo has challenging terrain, there are also moderate routes suitable for those looking for a less strenuous ride. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067466520/valle-del-lago-l-outeiro-climb" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Lago - L'Outeiro Climb&lt;/a&gt; (18.7 km) or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pola de Somiedo – Restaurante Casa Cobrana loop from Somiedo&lt;/a&gt; (17.1 km), both offering beautiful scenery without extreme difficulty.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I expect along the no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: Somiedo's routes offer breathtaking panoramic views. The climb to Alto de la Farrapona provides stunning vistas of the Saliencia Lakes. Another must-see is the Mirador del Príncipe (Prince's Viewpoint) near La Peral, which offers some of the best overall views of the natural park's rugged mountains and valleys.

Q: Are there any loop trails for traffic-free road cycling in Somiedo?
A: Yes, you can find loop trails that allow you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pola de Somiedo – Restaurante Casa Cobrana loop from Somiedo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 17.1 km route that circles back to Pola de Somiedo, the capital town.

Q: What cultural attractions or landmarks can I see near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Somiedo is rich in cultural heritage. You can encounter traditional "teito" huts and "brañas" (seasonal mountain pastures) scattered throughout the landscape. Pola de Somiedo, the capital, features traditional architecture, the San Pedro church, and the Somiedo Natural Park Interpretation Centre.

Q: Is parking available near the starting points of these no-traffic routes?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in the main towns and villages that serve as starting points for routes, such as Pola de Somiedo. It's advisable to check specific parking regulations or availability for smaller trailheads, especially during peak season.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the no-traffic routes in Somiedo?
A: The komoot community highly rates the road cycling experience in Somiedo, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the dramatic, quiet roads, the stunning glacial landscapes, and the challenging yet rewarding climbs that offer unparalleled views of the Asturian wilderness.

Q: Are there any specific regulations or permits required for cycling in Somiedo Natural Park?
A: Somiedo is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, and while general cycling on public roads is permitted, it's always good practice to respect park regulations. There are no specific permits required for cycling on the main roads, but always stay on marked paths and roads, and be mindful of wildlife and the natural environment.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to refuel along the no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: While many routes offer a sense of wilderness, you can find amenities in the main villages like Pola de Somiedo. Some routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pola de Somiedo – Restaurante Casa Cobrana loop&lt;/a&gt;, even incorporate stops at local establishments, allowing you to refuel and experience local cuisine.
tips: Near the beautiful town of Valle del Lago we are in the neighboring town of Auteiro. End of the ascent to the Fuente de L'Auteiro which is located at an altitude of 1,278 masl, a drop of 587 meters and a length of 9.10 km. The average % is 6.45 with a maximum of 16%.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Valle del Lago - L'Outeiro Climb for road cyclists?
faqs.answer: This climb is rated as moderate but presents a significant challenge due to its irregular terrain. While the average gradient is around 6.22% over 9 kilometers, you'll encounter consistent ramps of 7-8% in the initial kilometers, followed by sections frequently exceeding 10%, including one kilometer nearing an 11% incline. The gradient eases somewhat after 6 kilometers, but considerable 10-11% ramps persist. It's a demanding ascent that will test your cycling endurance.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 18.7 kilometers long (out-and-back) and typically takes around 1 hour and 13 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the L'Outeiro Climb?
faqs.answer: The route is entirely on asphalted road up to L'Outeiro. The road surface is generally in very good condition, making it suitable for race bikes. As you approach and pass through the village of Valle de Lago, the road narrows significantly. Beyond L'Outeiro, the path transitions to a dirt track towards Lago del Valle, which is not suitable for road bikes.

faqs.question: What are the parking options for starting this climb?
faqs.answer: The climb officially begins in Pola de Somiedo after crossing a small bridge. You can typically find parking in or near Pola de Somiedo to start your ride. Traffic on the route itself is generally light.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers breathtaking views of the Somiedo Natural Park's vibrant green landscapes. You'll pass through shaded sections with trees in the earlier portions of the climb and encounter several horseshoe curves. A notable feature along the way is the Fuente de L'Auteiro.

faqs.question: Is the Valle del Lago - L'Outeiro Climb dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Somiedo Natural Park is generally welcoming, this specific route is a road cycling climb. Dogs are typically not suitable companions for demanding road cycling ascents. If you plan to visit the park with a dog, it's best to research specific hiking trails that are appropriate for pets and ensure they are kept on a leash to protect local wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle in Somiedo Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route within the Somiedo Natural Park. However, as it is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, visitors are expected to respect the natural environment and adhere to general park regulations regarding wildlife, waste, and noise.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is best enjoyed during the warmer months, from late spring to early autumn, when the weather is more stable and the landscapes are lush. During these seasons, you'll experience pleasant temperatures and the full beauty of the Somiedo Natural Park. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter in Somiedo Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The Somiedo Natural Park is a natural paradise renowned for its rich biodiversity. While cycling, you might be lucky enough to spot various wildlife, including bears, wolves, golden eagles, and vultures. Remember to observe from a distance and never disturb the animals.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this road cycling route overlaps with sections of several other trails. Notably, it covers 100% of the [CIMA AS12] Valle del Lago - L'Outeiro route. It also briefly intersects with parts of the Transcantábrica and the Ruta de los Ḷḷagos.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to get refreshments near the route?
faqs.answer: Pola de Somiedo, where the climb officially begins, is a village where you can typically find cafes, restaurants, and possibly accommodation options before or after your ride. As you ascend, the route becomes more remote, so it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks.
